slots.meaning: The new night-shift hire whose choice of post and choices thereafter drive the whole story.
slots.guidance: A named new hire on her first overnight shift; observant, a little unsettled, never reckless for its own sake.

slots.meaning: The night supervisor who sorts new hires into the three posts at the hub node.
slots.guidance: A calm, veteran overnight supervisor, twelve years on the job; briefs the induction rules and assigns posts.

slots.meaning: The vanished worker every one of the three posts independently rediscovers; the mystery every track's endings resolve around.
slots.guidance: A single name for a former night-shift worker who signed in decades ago and was never signed out; referenced, half-seen, or found in records, never a menacing presence.

slots.meaning: The whole workplace the entire story is set inside; a records-and-holdings site that only comes alive overnight.
slots.guidance: A single named site/building; a place with an unlisted address, locked and dark by day, alive only for the overnight shift.

slots.meaning: The specific, numbered floor/level that the building's floor plans deny exists, and that all three tracks converge toward.
slots.guidance: A single named sub-level/floor designation; off-limits without authorization; wonder and unease over dread.

slots.meaning: The colloquial nickname for the sealed floor/its features, used adjectivally throughout all three tracks (e.g. as part of a camera, a door, a drawer).
slots.guidance: A short adjectival nickname for the sealed floor's chill; used constantly as a compound modifier, never on its own as the formal designation.

slots.meaning: The recurring visual warning cue (a corridor's color) that signals the building is actively searching/counting; the story's literal, escalating clock.
slots.guidance: A single warning color/signal word; gives fair warning (stand still until it clears), escalates gradually, never an instant hazard.

slots.meaning: The name of the overnight work shift the whole story takes place during; the story's title concept.
slots.guidance: A short, workplace-schedule term naming the overnight rotation.

slots.meaning: The identity object whose orientation (face out vs. face in) is the building's rule for who may be spoken to.
slots.guidance: A worn identity object with two readable faces; its orientation is a plain, non-violent social signal, never a weapon.

slots.meaning: The building's own nightly tally/audit mechanism, checking the living against its records; the story's central uncanny process.
slots.guidance: A short noun for a recurring tally/audit ritual; described as arithmetic and bookkeeping, not a hunt or a threat.

slots.meaning: The lead of the Security post (the camera wall); the hero's guide on that track.
slots.guidance: A veteran overnight camera-room watcher; calm, plainspoken, has kept this watch far longer than she says.

slots.meaning: The Security post's workspace: the room of monitors the hero watches from.
slots.guidance: A room lined with live monitors; the Security track's home base.

slots.meaning: The lead of the Archives post (the record vault); the hero's guide on that track.
slots.guidance: A veteran record-keeper; unhurried, precise, carries a private grief about one entry he has never closed.

slots.meaning: The Archives post's workspace: the vault of card records the hero files in.
slots.guidance: A long vault of record drawers under a single lamp; the Archives track's home base.

slots.meaning: The lead of the Maintenance post (the plant); the hero's guide on that track.
slots.guidance: A veteran plant engineer; matter-of-fact, protective of the new hire, quietly familiar with the building's stranger systems.

slots.meaning: The Maintenance post's workspace: the engineering spaces the hero tours and works in.
slots.guidance: The building's mechanical heart, generators and power bus and climate lines; the Maintenance track's home base.

slots.meaning: The Security track's blank-feed visual motif: the interference filling the sealed floor's camera.
slots.guidance: A camera-interference texture that reads as empty but is not; a visual, not a hazard.

slots.meaning: The permanent record the hero can choose to sign at the end of the Security track's amber-sweep branch.
slots.guidance: A formal witness record; signing it is the track's success-leaning choice.

slots.meaning: The specific stairwell landmark where the Security track's third branch (the flickering figure) plays out.
slots.guidance: A named stairwell/landing; a waypoint the hero can watch from a distance or go down to in person.

slots.meaning: The unofficial storage device holding the one surviving clip of the vanished worker, central to that branch's final choice.
slots.guidance: A private, off-the-books storage device; a benign object, the sole surviving evidence, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The Archives track's master record system; the abstraction the vault exists to maintain.
slots.guidance: A record-keeping system/book that is described as never wrong, only ever incomplete; a benign paper object, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The vanished worker's folded coat, the key found object in the Maintenance track's climax.
slots.guidance: A worn garment left behind; a benign, sorrowful keepsake, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The unexplained climate-control system keeping the sealed room comfortable; the Maintenance track's central mechanical mystery.
slots.guidance: A building climate-control subsystem; a benign mechanical detail, described in terms of comfort, not danger.

slots.meaning: The electrical breaker the hero and her guide must reset early in the Maintenance track.
slots.guidance: A single electrical panel/breaker fixture; a mundane mechanical obstacle.

slots.meaning: The building's freight lift, which stops answering after a fixed hour and forces the stairs.
slots.guidance: A single named freight conveyance; a mundane mechanical detail establishing the after-hours rule.
